semanticCore: Guarding, protecting, watching over, preserving, or shield
quranicRelevance: Who protects you at night and day? (Yakla'ukum - 'man yakla-ukum bil-layli').

keyDerivations.meaning: To guard, protect, watch over, shield, preserve, or shepherd

figurativeOrRareUsages: قُلْ مَنْ يَكْلَؤُكُمْ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ مِنَ الرَّحْمَٰنِ (21:42): Say: 'Who guards/protects you by night and by day from the (punishment of the) Most Merciful?'
discardedNoise: Green desert grass (Kala') - spelling overlap in dictionaries.
summaryEn: The root denotes protective guarding (Kilaa'ah), asking a rhetorical question to challenge humans on who preserves them from sudden cosmic harm other than God.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الحفظ والحراسة والرعاية الحذرة (كلأ)، سائلاً الكفار عمن يحفظهم ويحرسهم ليل نهار من بأس الرحمن غير الله (يكلوكم).
rawText: الكُلَى : the feathers so called: see مَنَاكِبُ and أَبْهَرُ. الكُلْيَتَانِ A name of two stars on the ear of Taurus. (See الكَلْبَانِ.) كَلَا 1 , first pers. كَلَاتُ, aor. يَكْلُو; and كَلَا, first pers. كَلَيْتُ, aor. يَكْلَا; inf. n., كِلَايَةٌ; see كَلَأَ.
